personaChecks.evidence: Front matter declares 'persona: business'. The page lives under business-users/website-content/ and contains UI-driven procedural content aimed at non-technical users. Single persona assignment is correct per INV-persona-002 (business guides → 'business').

The added callout uses plain, friendly language: 'To create a page, you need *Read* and *Create* page permissions on the parent page under which you want to place the new page.' No developer jargon (no ACL, API, SDK, code references). 'Read' and 'Create' are italicized UI-facing permission names, not technical terms. The sentence is direct, uses 'you', and keeps to one clear statement. Consistent with INV-persona-013 (friendly, positive tone; no special terminology; words understandable by a general audience).

The callout provides exactly the right depth for business users: it states what permissions are needed and links to the full permissions page for details. It does not explain the permission inheritance model, API internals, or configuration steps — those details are appropriately left to the linked developer/admin page. Business users learn what they need without being overwhelmed.

All terms used are either everyday English ('create a page', 'parent page', 'you need') or official product UI labels ('Read', 'Create', 'page permissions'). No specialized developer terminology. Meets INV-persona-007 (simple, ordinary, everyday words for non-native English speakers).

No developer or admin terminology leaks into this business-user page. The permission reference uses the same user-facing terminology as the sibling page edit-and-publish-pages.md (e.g., '*Read* and *Update* page permissions'). Meets INV-persona-016 (do not use terminology from another persona's domain).

classificationCheck.evidence: Page contains numbered, goal-oriented procedural steps for creating and cloning pages. The added callout is a prerequisite note within the procedure, not a conceptual explanation. Content matches 'howto' classification.

consistencyCheck.notes: All sibling pages in the Website Content topic cluster (delete-pages, edit-and-publish-pages, secure-pages, translate-pages, etc.) target 'business' persona. The edit-and-publish-pages.md page uses the identical pattern for permission callouts ('*Read* and *Update* page permissions' with page_link to permissions_pagelevel_xp). The create-pages callout follows this established convention. The only minor difference is the use of {% info %} vs {% note %} tags for the callout — this is a style concern outside persona review scope.

invariantResults.evidence: Front matter 'persona: business' uses a valid persona value from the allowed set (developer, admin, business, architect, all).
Business guide correctly assigned 'business' persona. No admin or architect content present that would require additional persona tags.
Content uses simple, ordinary words accessible to non-native English speakers. Permission names ('Read', 'Create') are product-specific terms clearly marked with italics.
Content is written with clear understanding of the business persona: task-oriented, UI-driven, no implementation details.
Friendly, positive tone. Single clear statement per callout. No special terminology. Words understandable by a general audience.

Content correctly assumes business users work on a team with technical people. The callout states the permission requirement and links to the full permissions page where admins can configure permissions — it doesn't ask the business user to configure permissions themselves.
No developer or admin terminology used in this business-user page. Permission names are product UI labels, not technical jargon.

This invariant applies at the task-set level. T-004 covers the business user scenario (permission callout on the create-pages page). The developer scenario is covered by T-001 (the permissions reference page). Individual pages correctly target their specific persona.
Front matter contains all required fields: title, identifier, order, redirect_from, persona, license.
